{"query":"Alaska man federal charges threats kill Supreme Court justices indictment status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1203,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://abc7ny.com/post/alaska-man-panos-anastasiou-charged-threatening-assassinate-us-supreme-court-justices/15330029/","title":"Alaska man, Panos Anastasiou, charged with threatening to assassinate US Supreme Court justices - ABC7 New York","content":"Alaska man charged with threatening to assassinate 6 Supreme Court justices
A grand jury handed up a 22-count indictment against the man, Panos Anastasiou, 76, on Tuesday, charging him with making threats against a federal judge and making threats in interstate commerce.
Anastasiou pleaded not guilty at a hearing in Alaska's federal court on Wednesday.
Anastasiou's threatening messages started in March 2023, according to prosecutors, and continued through mid-July.
A Supreme Court spokeswoman did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
"His racist, homophobic, vile rhetoric is meant to intimidate high level government officials from carrying out their official duties."},{"url":"https://alaskabeacon.com/briefs/anchorage-man-set-to-plead-guilty-for-violent-threats-against-us-supreme-court-justices/","title":"Anchorage man set to plead guilty for violent threats against US Supreme Court justices | Alaska Beacon","content":"An Anchorage man is set to plead guilty for making violent threats against U.S.
Panos Anastasiou, 77, was charged with 22 crimes in the U.S.
He was accused of sending 465 messages to the Supreme Court starting in March of 2023.
Federal prosecutors said the messages contained threats to kill six justices, alongside racist and homophobic rhetoric.
Attorney General Merrick Garland alleged that Anastasiou “made repeated, heinous threats to murder and torture Supreme Court Justices and their families to retaliate against them for decisions he disagreed with.”
The filing does not describe which charges he is set to plead guilty to.
Federal court filings did not identify the justices Anastasiou allegedly threatened.
A charge of making threats against a federal judge carries a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/alaska-man-charged-with-sending-graphic-threats-to-kill-supreme-court-justices/","title":"Alaska man charged with sending graphic threats to kill Supreme Court justices | Courthouse News Service","content":"Threats targeting federal judges overall have more than doubled in recent years amid a surge of similar violent messages directed at public officials around the country.
WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alaska man accused of sending graphic threats to injure and kill six Supreme Court justices and some of their family members has been indicted on federal charges, authorities said Thursday.
Panos Anastasiou, 76, is accused of sending more than 465 messages through a public court website, including graphic threats of assassination and torture coupled with racist and homophobic rhetoric.
The indictment does not specify which justices Anastasiou targeted, but Attorney General Merrick Garland said he made the graphic threats as retaliation for decisions he disagreed with.
Prosecutors said in an indictment filed Wednesday that the messages were sent between March 2023 and mid-July 2024.
Anastasiou was arrested Wednesday in Anchorage.

tavily.raw: {"query":"Alaska man federal charges threats kill Supreme Court justices indictment status","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/alaska-man-charged-with-making-threats-to-kill-supreme-court-justices","title":"Alaska man charged with making threats to kill Supreme Court justices | PBS News","content":"FILE PHOTO: The U.S. Supreme Court building in Washington. # Alaska man charged with making threats to kill Supreme Court justices. WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alaska man accused of sending graphic threats to injure and kill six Supreme Court justices and some of their family members has been indicted on federal charges, authorities said Thursday. Panos Anastasiou, 76, is accused of sending more than 465 messages through a public court website, including graphic threats of assassination and torture coupled with racist and homophobic rhetoric. Anastasiou has been charged with 22 counts, including nine counts of making threats against a federal judge and 13 counts of making threats in interstate commerce. He faces a maximum of 10 years in prison for each count of making threats against a federal judge and up to five years for each count of making threats in interstate commerce if convicted. An Alaska man has been indicted on charges that he sent racist and violent threats against six Supreme Court justices and their family members. “We allege that the defendant made repeated, heinous threats to murder and torture Supreme Court Justices and their families to retaliate against them for decisions he disagreed with,” Attorney General Merrick Garland said in a statement, adding that “democracy depends on the ability of public officials to do their jobs without fearing for their lives or the safety of their families.”. An Alaska man was charged and arrested Wednesday for sending numerous graphic and violent messages through a public Supreme Court communications portal that threatened to injure and kill six justices, according to court filings and the Justice Department. Some of the messages, according to court filings, used the N-word in threats to \"lynch\" a justice - identified in the filings as \"Supreme Court Justice 1\" - while also threatening the justice's \"insurrectionist wife.\" Other messages refer to shooting another justice - identified \"Supreme Court Justice 2\" - and killing his wife, and another alleged message threatened six justices total, saying that they should \"be AFRAID very AFRAID to leave their home and fear for their lives everyday.\". Anastasiou pleaded not guilty at a hearing in Alaska's federal court on Wednesday.","score":0.85186684,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://alaskabeacon.com/briefs/anchorage-man-set-to-plead-guilty-for-violent-threats-against-us-supreme-court-justices","title":"Anchorage man set to plead guilty for violent threats against US Supreme Court justices | Alaska Beacon","content":"Image 1: Alaska Beacon HomePart of States Newsroom. # Anchorage man set to plead guilty for violent threats against US Supreme Court justices. An Anchorage man is set to plead guilty for making violent threats against U.S. Supreme Court justices and their families. Panos Anastasiou, 77, was charged with 22 crimes in the U.S. District Court of Alaska in 2024. Then-U.S. Attorney General Merrick Garland alleged that Anastasiou “made repeated, heinous threats to murder and torture Supreme Court Justices and their families to retaliate against them for decisions he disagreed with.”. Anastasiou’s federal public defender and the U.S. Attorney for the District of Alaska did not immediately respond to a request for a copy of his written guilty plea agreement. by Sean Maguire, Alaska Beacon.
